chapters.title: Technology opens doors, but it’s not the only way forward
chapters.text:
The big technology companies, primarily based in Silicon Valley, have long been the industry leaders on the worldwide stage. We look toward them for new ideas, new products, and ways in which we can improve our lives and businesses for the better. It’s easy to understand why when you consider just how quickly technology develops. And with huge investment pouring in, it seems like there’s nothing we can’t do.

Of course, to use the most sophisticated technology means being able to afford it in the first place. You could argue that’s partly why large-scale businesses continue to get richer and smaller companies struggle so much. Technology costs money.

Dan Breznitz has long been an advocate for different types of innovation. He doesn’t think that we should always look toward international technology models and instead focus upon innovation we can create around us. Just because Apple is known to be the industry leader in computing, does that mean we always need to use Apple products? Just because Ford has long been known to be one of the best in the automotive industry, should we avoid any other company?

Technology allows us to dream the so-called impossible, but not everything needs to be copied or derived from Silicon Valley and its hugely successful tech companies.

Innovation in Real Places argues that we should look toward different types of development and assess what is best for us as a whole. Companies can make their own choices and focus upon their in-house innovation rather than always following a route set by Silicon Valley. The COVID-19 pandemic swept across the world and caused havoc over the last few years, and this has led us to question whether we should focus on development on quite so much of a global stage. In some cases, it just brings about more problems and roadblocks.

This summary will help you understand what innovation is and how it can be pulled back from a global reach and centered more locally and nationally. You’ll also realize that focusing solely on technology doesn’t necessarily have to be the way forward. By shifting our mindset away from the global aspect of the issue, perhaps we can empower local communities to create their own far-reaching success stories.

chapters.title: There is a fundamental difference between innovation and invention
chapters.text:
One of the biggest misconceptions is the difference between innovation and invention; many people assume them to be one and the same. We believe we first need to develop a brand new idea to be innovative, something nobody has ever thought of before. But, that’s not the case. Instead, innovation is about developing an existing idea, working to make something that’s already around into something better.

Dan Breznitz argues that this misconception has created significant problems for businesses over the years. Rather than thinking outside of the box and looking at how their existing models can be tweaked and improved, they pour vast amounts of cash into technology and come up with brand new ideas. For many businesses, that simply runs them into the ground or ultimately stunts their growth.

To understand why we can find innovation more locally than we assume, we need to understand the significant differences between innovation and invention. Of course, invention is coming up with a brand new idea. When Alexander Graham Bell invented the telephone, it was a unique idea that he hit upon and then developed. But innovation is different; it takes an existing idea and improves it.

Innovation is the complete process of taking new ideas and devising new or improved products and services. It comes in all stages from the first vision, design, development, production, sale, and usage, to the after-sale aspects of products and services. ~ Dan Breznitz

While we can’t remove the significant impact of new inventions, it’s essential to recognize that innovation is probably the actual winner. For instance, the car has already been invented and has been around for a long time. But, by innovating the idea and coming up with ways to make it better, we’re improving the lives of millions and perhaps saving money for businesses who choose to work with us.

Innovation is the story after invention.

Dan Breznitz states that the fake news society these days means that many people weave misinformation about innovation. They simply don’t understand the differences, so they don’t understand global production. All this does is create economic damage far more significant than we probably realize right now.

chapters.title: Local communities can be successful innovators too
chapters.text:
While Silicon Valley’s abundance of tech startups has certainly given us significant benefits over the years, assuming that copying their model for global growth is the only way forward has cost many businesses dearly. Attempting to emulate something else rarely goes well; instead, you need to think outside of the box and look toward your strengths.

How can a business improve its market reach and expand its influence on the global platform without assuming that technology and pouring endless cash into international endeavors is the way forward? It comes down to looking closer to home.

Dan Breznitz argues that local communities hold power here, but we need to open our eyes to what they can achieve. He believes that the single best way to move toward long-term growth in global economies and the quality of life is to look toward innovation rather than developing brand new ideas and becoming an inventor. He also believes that it has nothing to do with always creating new technological products. Instead, it is about developing ways to improve the products and services we already have. It’s all about specialized innovation.

It’s a sad truth that businesses the world over have wasted millions attempting to copy the Silicon Valley model.

We assume that innovation in technology and coming up with mind-blowing new tech ideas is the fast-track toward global success and growth.

To do this, you need to focus on education and research and partner up with the best tech companies around. But all of this is extremely expensive, and many countries are simply unable to do so, and we’re left with a select number of countries consistently succeeding while everyone else seems to be fighting a battle they have no chance of winning. We need to even the playing field lying within local communities rather than always looking toward Silicon Valley’s tech “miracles.”

chapters.title: The business world has its eyes closed to the significant potential for innovation all around them
chapters.text:
Within every single country, some individuals have the power to change the economy’s success. They have talents and ideas that we ignore because we assume that high-tech models are the route we need to follow.

There is massive potential everywhere, but most businesses have their eyes closed.

There are many options ahead that simply don’t require copying technological industries. By confusing innovation with invention, we’re missing the point entirely. Startups might look attractive on paper and seem glamorous in practice, but they’re creating a significant obstacle for the growth of other companies. Most countries and cities outside China, Japan, and the USA fail to succeed because they’re copying and not innovating.

National communities throw away time and money on literally nothing; instead, they could focus on their strengths and where they sensibly fit on the production process line.

Small and medium-sized businesses can catapult into the high-growth category with a laser focus on innovation. By taking what they already do and looking at ways to improve it locally, that business could enhance its success rate massively over a short amount of time. Growth can jumpstart and provoke significant support for those who need it by being more diverse in business decisions.

However, Dan Breznitz is keen to point out that not everything is grim and critical. We have come a long way over the last half a century. Technology has dominated, but it has served us well. We can also say that life expectancy has risen, and infant mortality has reduced. So, while not everything is about business success, we need to understand the impact of global success on the quality of life.

Did you know? According to Forbes, Mark Zuckerberg was the wealthiest person in Silicon Valley in 2021, with a net worth of $97 billion.

chapters.title: Production stage specialization is the key to future growth success
chapters.text:
We know that we should empower communities to create innovation, but how? Dan Breznitz believes that production stage specialization is the key to changing everything.

Rather than looking to create a brand-new product or trying to make an entire product better, focus on where you fit best in the production process of that particular product. For instance, rather than developing a brand-new type of car, focus on one part of that car that you can do well. Maybe you have a team around you that is highly knowledgeable about engines and, in that case, specialize in engine production rather than the entire car.

Breznitz highlights the ‘all-in’ mindset that used to be prevalent across global businesses. He gives Henry Ford as one key example. Ford cars were the cornerstone of success during the 1920s and 1930s. At this time, Ford decided that they needed to focus on every part of the production, and as such, they owned acres and acres of land, mines, and quarries across America. They also owned rubber plantations in Brazil for producing tires. Every part of that car was created and owned by Ford and developed on land and in factories that Ford owned.

This model is not only expensive, but it’s also time-consuming, and it simply doesn’t fit in with today’s manufacturing world. Very few companies can afford to create the same large-scale production process, owning land and factories as Ford did. So, we need to move away from assuming that we must control every part of a product and instead specialize in what we do best.

Specialization allows your business to become the very best in that particular area. It requires fewer skills, less capital, and less outlay while giving you a better chance of success.

Production stage specialization means you can become the expert on just one particular segment of the product. TSMC in Taiwan has managed to become successful by specializing in chip fabrication only and selling their chips worldwide. They didn’t need a massive amount of capital to innovate all parts of a whole; they simply needed to innovate the chip.

chapters.title: Empower local communities, and everyone benefits
chapters.text:
Until businesses wake up and realize that blindly looking toward inventing new technological products isn’t going to bring them instant success, nothing will change. Large companies will get richer, while small companies will continue to struggle. It also means that the world’s top tech power countries will become more powerful while other countries fall behind, affecting business and the quality of life for people who live there. We need to start leveling up the playing field.

By changing the structure of the production system worldwide, we can empower local communities to make significant changes. Countries that we currently don’t look toward for considerable business success stories will start creating them. We can level the playing field and take some of the power and wealth away from the extremely wealthy, distributing it more evenly to benefit everyone around the globe.

Silicon Valley continues to become richer with every passing year. We don’t need more technology; we need more specialization.

Local communities have more talent within them than we realize. By giving them the reins and empowering them with the chance to make significant waves in the business world, we move away from assuming that technology and invention are the be-all and end-all. Education, training, and putting faith in local talent are ways we can empower local communities and lead them toward new opportunities that have a significant impact on global production.

If there is one lesson that I hope you, the readers of this book, will take away from it, it is to believe in human ingenuity and the ability of communities to chart their own future even under the worst global headwinds. ~ Dan Breznitz

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
There’s no denying that technology is fantastic. It’s astonishing that the Internet didn’t go mainstream until the 1990s; we rely so much on the online world that it’s almost impossible to remember life without it.

All of this means that we cannot push aside the significant benefits of technology and what it has given to us. But, by assuming that every single thing has to connect to a new technological invention, we’re missing the point. Innovation doesn’t mean invention. We don’t need to keep coming up with new ideas; we simply need to develop the ideas we already have.

Innovation in Real Places reminds us that anyone can recreate the success of the famous startups in Silicon Valley. While the major players in San Francisco are becoming wealthier by the year, everyone else is falling behind. The sad thing is that it doesn’t have to be this way. Businesses have more talent at their fingertips than they realize. Local communities no doubt have endless options that they cannot see.

The answer is specialization. Don’t focus on being good at everything; instead, focus on the one thing you’re good at and push that to the extremes. You’ll save money and time and become far more successful. This benefits everyone, including increased job opportunities in the local community, cash injection locally, and a better quality of life overall. Business isn’t just about making the rich richer; it’s about benefiting everyone and creating opportunities for significant growth worldwide.

Try this

• What are you good at? Spend some time thinking about that question and how you can specialize your skills.
• Outsourcing major parts of your production process is expensive and time-consuming. What can you do in-house to save money?
• Be honest with yourself — are you trying to come up with a new idea from scratch? Remember that innovation isn’t about invention; it’s about development.

Read it till the end?
